  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,

  "confidence": 0.9,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Receptacle has an open ground — equipment grounding conductor is not connected or is absent at the device",
    "Missing or unconnected EGC renders the receptacle non-compliant with NEC 406.4 grounding requirements",
    "Open ground creates shock hazard by leaving exposed metal parts of connected equipment without fault current return path"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify equipment grounding conductor is present in the box and properly terminated at the receptacle grounding screw",
    "If EGC is absent, install a compliant ground or apply an approved NEC 406.4(D) exception if applicable",
    "Test receptacle with a 3-light tester or multimeter to confirm ground continuity before closing out",
    "Do not energize circuit until ground path is verified as continuous and compliant"
  ],
  "rationale": "An open ground on a residential receptacle is a code violation under NEC 406.4, which requires receptacles to be grounded where a grounding means exists. This is an installed-defective condition at rough-complete stage that must be corrected before closeout. The open ground eliminates the safety path for fault current, creating a significant shock hazard.",
  "references": ["NEC 406.4", "NEC 210.8", "NEC 406.9"]
}
